summary: No-ship: P-5 remains acceptance-bypassable, although no separate new critical issue was found. The committed RED witness and verify-record are valid. Simpler alternative: retain the approved B+A design but make W8(l) enforce the wrapper’s exact AST instead of adding further hooks.

findings.title: Test-only configuration can conceal the broken production recovery path
findings.body: W11 runs through `PassGuard::begin` with `pre_recover_grave = Some`, whereas production always uses `None`. W8(l) only counts one snapshot and one `recover_graves_from` call and forbids selected loop/syscall tokens; it does not require that delegation to be unconditional or prohibit another call. An implementation can therefore use the correct helper under `cfg(test)` or when the hook is present, while delegating production to an unscanned raw helper. W8, W11, and characterization tests remain green, but production still aborts when a snapshotted grave vanishes—the exact P-5 failure.
findings.recommendation: Make W8(l) parse or normalize the wrapper and require its complete body to be exactly an unconditional `Entry::snapshot` followed by the tail call to `recover_graves_from`. Reject conditional compilation, hook-presence branches, additional calls, and delegation. Mutation-check both `cfg(test)` and hook-presence variants.

next_steps: Strengthen W8(l) as recommended, record the configuration-skew mutant, and repeat only the P-5 review.
